spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates the exact CSS corner-shape contract: squircle, round, bevel, notch, scoop, superellipse(4), the four-value shorthand, corner-top-left-shape support, CSSOM round trips, and malformed superellipse() rejection.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome 148 exercised every visible shape, radius, and corner-set button. The requested value, CSSOM readout, computed corner-shape, pressed state, and previews update through the page's own click handlers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still compares squircle against round and superellipse UI components, and now also covers the per-corner mix and malformed-value behavior promised by the feature page.

edge_cases.rationale: The malformed control drives corner-shape: superellipse(foo); Chrome rejects it through CSS.supports and CSSOM, while the preview keeps a valid squircle fallback instead of hiding the failure.

references.rationale: The concept links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5357329815699456 and the CSS Borders Level 4 corner-shaping section.

design_system.rationale: Static scan found no raw hex colors, no source inline style attributes, no inline event handlers, no DOM markup assignment, and no legacy demo color aliases. Runtime interaction left document.querySelectorAll('[style]').length at 0.

summary: Repaired and rechecked with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148. The concept exposes live controls for shape values, radius values, mixed corners, CSSOM/computed readouts, malformed-value rejection, ChromeStatus/spec references, and the feature conformance route reports 10 pass / 0 fail.
